meaning_english: To cite as evidence or proof in an argument. Synonyms: Cite, Quote, Instance.
To produce something quickly and without much care. Synonyms: Crank out, Dash off.
To work very hard and steadily at something for a long time. Synonyms: Toil, Slave away.
To provide a warning or specific conditions to an agreement. Synonyms: Caution, Admonish.
To join a conversation by providing your own opinion. Synonyms: Interrupt, Interject.
To suddenly stop talking, especially because of nervousness or secrecy. Synonyms: Go silent, Dumbstruck.
To make a feeling or situation less intense or wild. Synonyms: Subdue, Restrain, Quell.
To incite or strongly encourage someone to do something foolish. Synonyms: Instigate, Goad.
To manage to make a living or a resource last with great effort. Synonyms: Scrimp, Subsist.
To praise someone excessively to get their favor. Synonyms: Flatter, Grovel, Bootlick.
To pay a large amount of money for something, usually reluctantly. Synonyms: Shell out, Cough up.
To prepare oneself for a difficult task or a fight. Synonyms: Prepare, Brace, Ready.
To mention or remember something from the past. Synonyms: Recall, Revert, Reminisce.
To separate part of a company or organization into a new one. Synonyms: Spin off, Segregate.
To resolve minor problems or settle difficulties. Synonyms: Resolve, Straighten out.
To fall over suddenly, especially because of feeling ill. Synonyms: Faint, Collapse.
To accept someone else's authority or give in to pressure. Synonyms: Submit, Yield, Succumb.
To accept something eagerly and with obvious pleasure. Synonyms: Gulp, Devour, Relish.
To be completely honest and truthful with someone. Synonyms: Be frank, Open up.
To waste time doing silly or unimportant things. Synonyms: Fool around, Mess about.
To look for something private or hidden. Synonyms: Pry, Snoop, Meddle.
To get rid of something by giving it to someone else dishonestly. Synonyms: Foist, Deceive.
To hide a problem rather than solving it properly. Synonyms: Conceal, Gloss over.
To be in agreement or square with something. Synonyms: Coincide, Match.
To control or limit someone or something. Synonyms: Restrain, Curb, Control.
To dishonestly take money from an organization over time. Synonyms: Embezzle, Drain.
To help someone through a difficult period (usually with money). Synonyms: Support, Sustain.
To improve the appearance of something to make it more attractive. Synonyms: Revamp, Refurbish.
To remove people or things from a group until only the best are left. Synonyms: Filter, Sift.
To focus all your attention on one particular thing. Synonyms: Pinpoint, Focus.
